_comment: WORKED EXAMPLE — Miguel's output = the MISMATCH, computed from beliefs (assertions) vs signals (events).
_status: DRAFT — the shape below is a proposal; the team finalizes the DriftAlert schema first (see shared/schemas/README.md).
_note: This is NOT fetched from anywhere — it is DERIVED by comparing data/customers/gentwo-ag.json against data/fixtures/gentwo-events.example.json.
_framing: NEUTRAL: 'material change -> re-KYC needed', NOT wrongdoing. A human analyst decides.

alert.flag: Business Activity Change + Geographic Expansion (Scale Risk)
alert.rationale: Onboarded as a Switzerland-only securitization fintech with no material crypto line (A5, A4). Public record now shows UK expansion (2024), a crypto joint-offering with a regulated digital-asset bank (2025), a Bitcoin structured product (2025), and on-chain tokenization (2026). The onboarding KYC scope is materially out of date.
alert.what_would_flip: Confirmation the digital-asset activity is within licensed scope and the new geographies are covered by the existing risk assessment.
alert.recommended_action: Refresh KYC scope; reassess activity profile & transaction-monitoring thresholds.
alert._open_question: One alert per contradicted assertion, or ONE alert per customer aggregating several? (kickoff — UX vs precision tradeoff)
